steps.title: Set up the similarity ratio using the intercept theorem
steps.content: Since $\overline{LM} \parallel \overline{PQ}$ and $\overline{LQ}$ intersects $\overline{MP}$ at R, triangles LRM and QRP are similar by AA (vertical angles at R; alternate interior angles with the parallel lines). Therefore: $$\frac{LR}{RQ} = \frac{MR}{RP} \implies \frac{10}{RQ} = \frac{9}{19} \implies RQ = \frac{190}{9}$$

steps.title: Compute LQ and test all choices
steps.content:
$LQ = LR + RQ = 10 + \frac{190}{9} = \frac{90}{9} + \frac{190}{9} = \frac{280}{9}$.
  • Choice A — $\frac{280}{19}$: Uses 19 as the denominator instead of 9; incorrect ratio application. ✗
  • Choice B — $\frac{190}{9}$: This is only RQ, forgetting to add LR = 10. ✗
  • Choice C — $\frac{271}{9}$: Does not correspond to any valid calculation. ✗
  • Choice D — $\frac{280}{9}$: $LR + RQ = 10 + \frac{190}{9} = \frac{280}{9}$. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
